jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature opening exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase delivery
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, culturally iconic

jokes.text: Ah, spring break in Hong Kong. Custom-made suits at slave-labor prices.
jokes.setup: Romanticizing spring break vacation
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions exploitation for cheap suits
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor about exploitation, good economy but could be from any show

jokes.text: G.M. Chrysler! I can't afford that!
jokes.setup: Skinner reacting to expensive flight changes
jokes.punchline: His censored swearing using car company names
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Skinner's uptight character - creative censored swearing that feels earned

jokes.text: Or can I? Think, Seymour, think.
jokes.setup: Skinner realizes he can't close school early for his vacation
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts scheming to do exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Skinner's self-serving nature

jokes.text: Go To Work With Your Parents Day
jokes.setup: Skinner needs to close school early for his vacation
jokes.punchline: Invents fake educational day to justify it
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven scheme, shows Skinner's bureaucratic creativity

jokes.text: Because I've always been an advocate of women in the workplace, Lise. I can't help it if Mom's workplace contains our TV.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning why Bart gets to stay home
jokes.punchline: Bart's clever rationalization for watching TV
jokes.explanation: Clever setup/payoff showing Bart's manipulative intelligence, good misdirection

jokes.text: Please note: Homemaker is not allowed as it is not real work. That's why you don't get paid for it.
jokes.setup: Official school form about parent occupations
jokes.punchline: Brutal dismissal of homemaking as work
jokes.explanation: Dark social commentary but feels more mean than clever

jokes.text: Some days we don't let the line move at all. We call those 'weekdays.'
jokes.setup: Explaining DMV operations to Bart
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of deliberate inefficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Patty/Selma's cruel bureaucratic characters, relatable DMV frustration

jokes.text: But where do they make those crackers with the peanut butter already inside? - Uh, put this on and come with me, son. - Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Milhouse asks innocent question about cracker production
jokes.punchline: Worker's ominous response suggests industrial secrets
jokes.explanation: Good mysterious industrial joke with Milhouse's naive enthusiasm

jokes.text: This has purple stuff inside. Purple is a fruit.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks for fruit, Homer offers doughnut
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - absurd reasoning delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: 'Cause they won't hire an assistant.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks why so many nuclear plant bulbs are burnt out
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact response about understaffing
jokes.explanation: Simple workplace humor, fits Homer's job but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Martin, you're up one million dollars. Yes! But now you've lost all but $600. You got greedy, Martin.
jokes.setup: Martin investing $10 in futures market
jokes.punchline: Rapid rise and fall, teaching harsh lesson
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing financial volatility, teaches Martin character lesson

jokes.text: Houston- We have a problem. Homer 13 is spinning out of control! I'm going after him!
jokes.setup: Homer and Lisa trying to make work fun
jokes.punchline: Playing astronauts with nuclear plant equipment
jokes.explanation: Creative father-daughter bonding through imagination, good visual element

jokes.text: Boy, this is a lot more fun with a second person.
jokes.setup: Homer and Lisa playing in the nuclear plant
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting his job is usually boring and lonely
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Homer's loneliness and love for Lisa

jokes.text: Hot damn! No more sittin' in the dirt at the drive-in.
jokes.setup: Nelson getting his driver's license
jokes.punchline: His specific motivation reveals low-class background
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Nelson's tough background, economical storytelling

jokes.text: Yes, you're right, Milhouse. It is a fake, which makes it a fake I.D.
jokes.setup: Milhouse pointing out the ID says age 25
jokes.punchline: Bart's circular logic explanation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Bart's quick thinking and confidence, good structure

jokes.text: I can think of at least two things wrong with that title.
jokes.setup: Looking at inappropriate movie title
jokes.punchline: Bart's analytical criticism
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about movie titles, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: We could go rent a carpet shampooer.
jokes.setup: Boys looking for something exciting to do with fake ID
jokes.punchline: Martin's completely mundane suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character moment - completely missing the point of teenage rebellion

jokes.text: Like there's any way to get some kicks with a big wad of cash and a license to drive- Wow.!
jokes.setup: Bart sarcastically dismissing Martin's money and driving ability
jokes.punchline: Suddenly realizes the potential
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure as Bart talks himself into the idea

jokes.text: Maybe because you are, as we say in Latin, a 'dorkus malorkus.'
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why she didn't hear about grammar competition
jokes.punchline: Bart's fake Latin insult
jokes.explanation: Creative fake Latin, very quotable and fits Bart's mean-spirited humor

jokes.text: That's not Latin. Mom, Bart's faking it!
jokes.setup: Bart's fake Latin insult
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately calls it out
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Lisa's intelligence, but predictable response

jokes.text: Oh, honey, you've had your glory. Now it's Bart's turn.
jokes.setup: Lisa protesting Bart's obvious lie
jokes.punchline: Marge's misplaced fairness
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - well-meaning but misguided parental fairness

jokes.text: He sure did.
jokes.setup: Nelson complaining that Nelson hit him
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan humor showing Bart's indifference to Milhouse's suffering

jokes.text: Oh.! Diablo Canyon 2... why can't you be more like Diablo Canyon 1?
jokes.setup: Homer dealing with nuclear plant problems
jokes.punchline: Treating power plants like disappointing children
jokes.explanation: Creative anthropomorphization showing Homer's parental perspective on everything

jokes.text: Sure, I would. I can always learn about owls this summer at bird camp.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Lisa wants another day at the plant
jokes.punchline: Lisa's nerdy alternative plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - choosing education over fun, bird camp is wonderfully nerdy

jokes.text: We're not going to a grammar rodeo. - That was just an alibi, Milton. - Martin.
jokes.setup: Martin asking about the grammar rodeo
jokes.punchline: Bart can't even remember Martin's name
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Bart's dismissive attitude toward Martin

jokes.text: We're not going to a grammar rodeo. That was just an alibi, Milton. Martin.
jokes.setup: Martin excited about grammar rodeo destination
jokes.punchline: Bart revealing the lie and getting Martin's name wrong
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment with added insult of wrong name

jokes.text: What's inside? - An information desk! - Cool!
jokes.setup: Building up the mystery of what's inside the sun sphere
jokes.punchline: Completely mundane answer that Bart finds exciting
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect anticlimatic punchline and Bart's genuine enthusiasm

jokes.text: The way I figure it... if the candy stays in the machine for more than a year, it's up for grabs.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting getting lunch
jokes.punchline: His made-up rule about vending machine candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - creating arbitrary rules to justify questionable behavior

jokes.text: Zagnut bar, Mr. Smithers? Razzles, Skittles, Whatchamacallits, Twizzlers? They all have hilarious names and are delicious.
jokes.setup: Homer offering candy to Smithers
jokes.punchline: His innocent appreciation of candy names
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment - finding genuine joy in simple things

jokes.text: It'll be just like the time they kicked me out of the sporting goods store.
jokes.setup: Homer describing slumber party plans
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to past embarrassing incident
jokes.explanation: Good background gag suggesting Homer's history of inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: That's it! Back to Winnipeg!
jokes.setup: Parent threatening unruly kids in car
jokes.punchline: Threatening to go to boring Canadian city
jokes.explanation: Funny choice of threat destination, Winnipeg as punishment is creative

jokes.text: Well, I didn't think I was rehabilitated, but, uh... I guess they needed the extra bed.
jokes.setup: Boys pick up hitchhiker
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of being released from prison early
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good economy, highlights danger the boys don't recognize

jokes.text: Branson, Missouri. My dad says it's like Vegas if it were run by Ned Flanders.
jokes.setup: Boys arriving in Branson
jokes.punchline: Perfect comparison explaining the town's nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures Branson's wholesome entertainment culture with excellent economy

jokes.text: Who do you love most- me, Bart or Maggie? - D'oh! All right. Dare.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks impossible father question
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately switches to dare to avoid answering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting moment - avoiding difficult emotional terrain with his signature exclamation

jokes.text: Dad, you tell everybody everything. Even Moe knew when I threw up on the dentist.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he can keep secrets
jokes.punchline: Lisa citing specific embarrassing example
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke showing Homer's inability to keep secrets

jokes.text: I like... Langdon Alger. - I have no idea who that is. - Nobody does. He's very quiet and enjoys puzzles.
jokes.setup: Lisa revealing her crush
jokes.punchline: The boy is completely unremarkable and unknown
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - attracted to intellectual, introverted boy, good setup/payoff

jokes.text: You're 14 years too late. - But there's another world's fair coming soon, right? - Before Friday?
jokes.setup: Boys arriving at defunct World's Fair
jokes.punchline: Bart's increasingly desperate questions
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as their plan falls apart, escalating desperation

jokes.text: You mean the wig sphere. You're welcome to go up there... if you want to see 16,000 boxes of unsold wigs.
jokes.setup: Boys asking about the famous sun sphere
jokes.punchline: It's now storage for massive wig inventory
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific image, good misdirection from the iconic sun sphere

jokes.text: I must have spent our last $10 on this Al Gore doll. - You are hearing me talk.
jokes.setup: Boys discovering they're broke
jokes.punchline: Martin's terrible purchase and the doll's boring catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good period political humor and perfect Martin character moment with terrible spending choice

jokes.text: But on the plus side, I knocked over the sun sphere. - Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Boys listing their terrible situation
jokes.punchline: Nelson finds silver lining in destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment - finding joy in destruction even in crisis

jokes.text: This never would've happened if we'd gone to Macon, Georgia. I'm just sayin', is all.
jokes.setup: Boys stranded and broke
jokes.punchline: Martin's passive-aggressive I-told-you-so
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier moment and fits Martin's vindictive nerd character

jokes.text: These lenses are perfect. Now I can rebottom those antique Coke bottles.
jokes.setup: Milhouse's glasses being stolen
jokes.punchline: Specific use for the thick lenses
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific use for Milhouse's thick glasses, good character detail

jokes.text: I knew it! I knew it! Because why would they have our national grammar rodeo in another nation?
jokes.setup: Bart reveals there's no grammar rodeo
jokes.punchline: Lisa's logical reasoning for why she was suspicious
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa moment showing her logical thinking and why the lie didn't work on her

jokes.text: No, that's a terrier. They're dogs.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting courier as travel option
jokes.punchline: Correcting Homer's mishearing
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Simpson, how would you like to escort 500 Big Macs to Marlon Brando's island?
jokes.setup: Bart looking for courier work
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific delivery request
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific request that sounds like real celebrity excess

jokes.text: Bart rented a car with a phony driver's license... and drove Milhouse, Nelson and Martin to a wig outlet in Knoxville... and the car got crushed, and they're out of money and they can't get home... and Bart's working as a courier and just came back from Hong Kong.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining Bart's situation
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd chain of events delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing how far things have spiraled, Lisa's delivery is perfect

jokes.text: I will send Bart the money to fly home, then I will murder him.
jokes.setup: Homer processing Bart's situation
jokes.punchline: Logical parental response escalates to violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental response structure - practical then emotional, good escalation

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Oh, God, no! Oh, this can't be happening! You're operating without a T-437, Springfield! Oh, sweet mother of mercy! I mean- I mean, my God!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to order nuclear equipment
jokes.punchline: Worker's escalating panic over safety violation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing nuclear safety panic, builds tension effectively

jokes.text: Forget the name Homer Simpson. Have it sent care of Langdon Alger.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning Homer not to use his real name
jokes.punchline: Homer uses Lisa's crush's name instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Homer did remember Lisa's secret, good character moment

jokes.text: It's gettin' too commercial. They've forgotten it's supposed to be about the grammar.
jokes.setup: Bart pretending to have been to grammar rodeo
jokes.punchline: Fake complaint about commercialization
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of cultural complaints about commercialization, Bart's commitment to the lie

jokes.text: I don't know where to begin telling you what's wrong with that.
jokes.setup: Getting calls about Bart's various misadventures
jokes.punchline: Marge's overwhelmed but understated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - measured but completely overwhelmed by the situation
